seoTitle: Hodgson vs Holland Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hodgson and Holland Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hodgson vs Holland Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hodgson and Holland Park.
heading: Hodgson vs Holland Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hodgson, Queensland and Holland Park, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hodgson has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Holland Park (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hodgson or Holland Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hodgson has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Holland Park scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hodgson scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Holland Park scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hodgson or Holland Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hodgson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
